title: UPA Lightweight Vendor Whitelist
description: UPA-1.2 compliant vendor whitelist schema for non-ERP deployments. Implements the deterministic counterparty validation model defined in Section 5.1 of the Universal Payment Agent standard.

whitelist_version.description: Semantic version of this whitelist configuration

mandate_currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code governing all transaction limits in this whitelist

last_authorized_by.description: Identity of the human principal who last authorized this whitelist via multi-signature cryptographic protocol

last_authorized_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of last multi-signature authorization

approved_counterparties.description: Keyed map of approved vendor configurations. Keys are internal vendor identifiers (e.g. VND-001).

CounterpartyEntry.description: A single approved counterparty configuration block enforced by the Fiduciary Translation Engine

legal_name.description: Full legal entity name as registered. Must match ERP or government registry records.

legal_entity_identifier.description: ISO 17442 Legal Entity Identifier (LEI). Strongly recommended; required for cross-border RTGS transactions.

routing_bic.description: ISO 9362 Business Identifier Code (BIC/SWIFT) for settlement routing. Must match the verified master registry.

account_iban.description: IBAN destination account. Optional for ACH/domestic non-IBAN rails; required for SEPA and SWIFT CBPR+ transactions.

max_single_transaction_limit.description: Maximum value permitted per individual transaction in the mandate_currency. The Fiduciary Layer enforces a hard block above this threshold.

max_monthly_aggregate_limit.description: Optional cumulative monthly cap across all transactions to this counterparty. If omitted, only per-transaction limits apply.

items.description: ISO 20022 ExternalPurpose1Code values permitted for this counterparty (e.g. COMP, TRAD, SALA, GDSV)

permitted_purpose_codes.description: Whitelist of ISO 20022 purpose codes. The Fiduciary Translation Engine rejects any transaction whose mapped purpose code is not in this list.

permitted_jurisdictions.description: Optional restriction to specific TargetJurisdictionString values. If omitted, all registered jurisdictions are permitted.

requires_human_approval_above.description: Optional threshold above which the Approval and Exception layer routes the transaction to human review, even if below max_single_transaction_limit.

active.description: If false, the Fiduciary Layer treats this counterparty as suspended and blocks all transactions without deleting the record.

notes.description: Free-text audit notes. Not processed by the Fiduciary Layer; for human review only.
